mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate profit margins, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of monthly observations where UNRATE crosses above the 6.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer spending and construction activity, which amplifies revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the interaction term in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of observations during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 230.0 while the federal funds rate stays below 0.5%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting market repricing of monetary policy tightening risks and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The negative return signal disappears when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0 and FEDFUNDS is low, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly expands household income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases correlate with negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP exceeds 13500.0 billion chained dollars, robust economic expansion validates earnings forecasts and reduces recession risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters where GDPC1 surpasses the 13500.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and a decline in the unemployment rate signals a synchronized labor and output recovery, which triggers rapid equity repricing and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive returns in less than 50% of cases when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls concurrently over a rolling 2-year window.
